description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a large, convex glass screen set within a thick, black plastic bezel. The front panel features a vertical array of rotary dials and push buttons on the right side, likely for channel selection, volume, and power, with a metallic accent around the controls. The casing is predominantly a dark, faux-wood veneer, giving it a classic, retro appearance, while the back is made of molded black plastic with ventilation slats and embossed warning labels. The television is boxy and robust, with a flat top and sides, and is designed to sit on a flat surface such as a table or TV stand. The overall construction suggests durability and weight, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century home electronics. No visible brand markings are discernible, and the screen is non-flat, indicating pre-LCD/LED technology.
description: A vintage CRT television with a convex glass screen, faux-wood veneer casing, and a cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the front right panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, thick black bezel, and a vertical cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth, faux-wood veneer panel with no controls or features visible.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the molded black plastic back panel with ventilation slats and embossed warnings, as well as the main access panel.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the faux-wood veneer panel, similar to the left, with some surface marks but no controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel with a matte finish, rectangular shape, and metallic accent around the control panel.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the front right vertical panel.
